Walk cost ~50–100ms per validator × ≥10 redundant walks.\\n   - Design: pre-load step writes `tools/scripts/_cache/workspace-index.json` (agents + skills + instructions + parsed frontmatter). `workspace-index.mjs` gains a `--use-cache` mode that loads from JSON if mtime ≥ scanned files. CI `validate:_node` runs a `prevalidate:cache-warm` step once before `run-p`, then deletes cache after.\\n   - Alternative: mega-validator approach — combine the ~10 agent-reading validators into a single multi-pass process. Higher refactor cost, but eliminates parallel-walk waste entirely.\\n   - Recommend: shared cache (lower risk, smaller refactor). Document mega-validator as alternative.\\n   - **Savings estimate**: 500ms–2s wall-clock per `validate:_node` run. The \\\"~40\\\" approximation is replaced by a real count + the `npm-run-all2` max-parallel default (verify in the installed copy under `node_modules/npm-run-all2/`).\\n4. **Independently verify alias-cluster claims** (review finding M3) — open each canonical script, find the `--only=` parser, list every switch arm:\\n   - [validate-artifacts.mjs](tools/scripts/validate-artifacts.mjs)\\n   - [validate-agents.mjs](tools/scripts/validate-agents.mjs)\\n   - [validate-skills.mjs](tools/scripts/validate-skills.mjs)\\n5. **Confirm orphan candidates** — final grep across `.github/`, `tools/`, `package.json`, `lefthook.yml`, `*.md`, `*.yml`, including these previously-unchecked locations:\\n   - `tools/apex-prompts/`\\n   - `tools/tests/`\\n   - `site/src/content/docs/`\\n   - `agent-output/`\\n   - `.archive/` (if present)\\n   Targets: `markdown-prettifiers/`, `validate-context-budget.mjs`, `strip-handoff-kind.py`, `migrate-legacy-findings.mjs`.\\n6. **Cross-check shared-walk hypothesis** — confirm [validate-deprecated-models.mjs](tools/scripts/validate-deprecated-models.mjs) walks its own tree (does not use [_lib/workspace-index.mjs](tools/scripts/_lib/workspace-index.mjs)). *Already confirmed during exploration; re-cite line number in the doc.*\\n7. **Read every lint config end-to-end** (review finding S2) — `.markdownlint-cli2.jsonc`, `eslint.config.mjs`, `.prettierrc.json`, `.prettierignore`, `.yamllint.yml`, `commitlint.config.js`. The earlier \\\"no contradictions\\\" assertion was inherited from a subagent that admitted only partial reading; the doc must either back the claim with file-line citations or downgrade §5 to \\\"inventory only, contradiction-check deferred.\\\"\\n8. **Decide Python-validator scope** (review finding S1) — explicitly mark in/out of scope:\\n   - `validate_orchestrator_handoff.py`\\n   - `validate_question_batching.py`\\n   - `validate_review_ceiling.py`\\n   - `smoke_verify.py`\\n   - `profile_debug_log.py`\\n   Recommendation: **inventory only** in §3, full retire/consolidate analysis deferred to a sibling plan. State this in the doc's TL;DR.\\n\\n## Phase 2 — Author doc\\n\\nSections mirror the skills-assessment template, with the corrections below.\\n\\n1. **TL;DR** — headline counts (from Phase 1 measurements, not approximations) + the §8 execution trigger up front. Cross-link to [docs/skills-assessment-2026-05-17.md](docs/skills-assessment-2026-05-17.md) explicitly (review finding G2).\\n2. **Inventory** — every `tools/scripts/*` file, every npm script grouped by canonical script invoked, every lint config, every CI workflow + lefthook hook caller, Python validators called out explicitly per Phase 1.8.\\n3. **§1 Retire** — table for the 4 orphan targets, action = `git rm`. Note paired deletion of [.markdownlint-cli2.jsonc](.markdownlint-cli2.jsonc#L174) ignore entry.\\n4. **§2 Dedupe aliases** — artifacts 5→2 (`validate:artifacts` + `fix:artifacts`); agents retire `lint:agent-checks` and `lint:model-alignment`. P3 entry: retire public `check:h2-order` npm script.\\n5. **§3 Consolidate** — selected items + a **\\\"Considered but rejected\\\"** subsection (review finding S7) recording why workflow-graph, IaC contract validators, heading/H2 cluster, and orphaned-content/glob-audit were not merged. One-line rationale per item.\\n   - **§3a Skill validators** 2→1 (merge [validate-skill-checks.mjs](tools/scripts/validate-skill-checks.mjs) into [validate-skills.mjs](tools/scripts/validate-skills.mjs)).\\n   - **§3b Model validators** 3→1 (`validate-model.mjs --only=`).\\n   - **§3c Considered but rejected**: workflow-graph (separate gates), IaC contract (schema vs prose), H2/heading cluster (refactor risk > benefit), orphaned-content vs glob-audit (different walk semantics).\\n6. **§4 Performance** — restructured per review findings M1, S3, S4, S5:\\n   - **§4a Root cause** — `validate:_node` runs N validators via `run-p`. Each is a separate Node.js process. The module-level cache in [workspace-index.mjs](tools/scripts/_lib/workspace-index.mjs) is per-process. At least 9 validators walk the agents tree independently (cite count from Phase 1.3).\\n   - **§4b Approach A — single-process mega-validator** (S4 promoted to equal weight): one Node entry-point that requires all validators as modules, shares one in-memory `workspace-index`. Trade-off: validators lose independent runnability. Refactor cost: medium.\\n   - **§4c Approach B — shared on-disk cache** at `tools/scripts/_cache/workspace-index.json`, warmed by a pre-step. Trade-off: invalidation complexity (stale cache, atomic writes). Refactor cost: small per-validator change, larger pre-step.\\n   - **§4d Cache invalidation algorithm** (review finding S3 — write it concretely or label as design proposal): mtime check against the newest file across all scanned dirs (agents, skills, instructions). Atomic write via `fs.writeFileSync(tmp); fs.renameSync(tmp, final)`. CI cache invalidated per workflow run unless `actions/cache` is wired to a content-addressed key (`hashFiles('.github/agents/**', '.github/skills/**', '.github/instructions/**')`). If any of these are unresolvable today, **label §4d as \\\"design proposal — implementation deferred.\\\"**\\n   - **§4e Savings split — CI vs local** (S5): on-disk cache helps local repeat invocations during dev (~100–500 ms per warm run). On CI, the savings depend entirely on whether `actions/cache` is wired in. Mega-validator approach saves both equally.\\n   - **§4f Honest range** (M1): all savings quoted as a range bracketed by the Phase 1.2 cold-vs-warm microbenchmark. If OS page cache already absorbs the redundant walks, the doc must say so.\\n7. **§5 Lint configs** — downgraded to \\\"inventory + observations\\\" (review finding S2). Claim non-contradiction **only** for configs read end-to-end in Phase 1.7; otherwise mark \\\"not checked\\\" explicitly. No assertion without evidence.\\n8. **§6 CI vs lefthook duplication map** — confirm two-pass model is intentional. No action.\\n9. **§7 Priority table** — ranked P1/P2/P3 actions with effort/savings/risk grades.\\n10. **§8 Execution trigger** (review finding S6 — new section): the doc is **input to a follow-up PR**, not the end state. Concrete gate:\\n    - User opens `chore/retire-orphan-scripts` PR within 7 days, **or**\\n    - Doc is reclassified as \\\"exploratory / not actioned\\\" in its revision footer.\\n    Without §8, the doc joins `skills-assessment-2026-05-17.md` on the unactioned shelf.\\n\\n## Phase 3 — Lint + handoff\\n\\n1. `npm run lint:md` on the new doc.\\n2. `01-requirements.md`.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83d\udc8e Executive Summary\\n\\n`smoke-test` is an internal HR data-capture web application (Node.js on\\nLinux App Service) for an internal workforce of <100 daily users with\\n25-100 concurrent peaks. Step 1 selected `dev` environment scope only;\\nproduction go-live requires a later approval gate. The architecture is a\\nmanaged-PaaS Azure web tier (App Service) fronted by Microsoft Entra ID\\nworkforce SSO, persisting HR form records to Azure SQL Database (General\\nPurpose Serverless), with attachments in zone-redundant Blob Storage and\\nsecrets in Key Vault. Observability uses Application Insights + a Log\\nAnalytics workspace, with an Action Group routing budget alerts to the\\nAzure subscription `Owner` role per the enforced cost-monitoring mode.\\n\\nPrimary optimization is **Security** (HR personal data + GDPR), with a\\ndeliberate trade-off in **Reliability** \u2014 Basic-tier App Service is\\nselected for the dev environment despite carrying no platform SLA. The\\ntarget 99.9% SLA is met for SQL (GP Serverless 99.99%) and Storage\\n(ZRS 99.9%); when the `prod` environment is added, App Service must move\\nto Standard S1 or Premium v3 to inherit a contractual SLA.\\n\\n### Recommended Architecture\\n\\n```mermaid\\nflowchart TB\\n    User[\\\"\ud83d\udc64 Internal Workforce<br/>Entra ID SSO\\\"] --> App[\\\"\ud83d\udcbb App Service<br/>Linux B2 (Node.js)\\\"]\\n    App --> KV[\\\"\ud83d\udd11 Key Vault<br/>(secrets)\\\"]\\n    App --> DB[(\\\"\ud83d\uddc4\ufe0f Azure SQL Database<br/>GP Serverless 2 vCore\\\")]\\n    App --> Storage[\\\"\ud83d\udce6 Blob Storage<br/>Standard ZRS\\\"]\\n    App --> AI[\\\"\ud83d\udcca Application Insights\\\"]\\n    AI --> LA[\\\"\ud83d\udcca Log Analytics Workspace\\\"]\\n    LA --> AG[\\\"\ud83d\udd14 Action Group<br/>(Owner role)\\\"]\\n\\n    subgraph \\\"Private Networking (PEs)\\\"\\n        DB\\n        Storage\\n        KV\\n    end\\n\\n    subgraph \\\"Identity\\\"\\n        Entra[\\\"\ud83e\udeaa Microsoft Entra ID<br/>Workforce SSO\\\"]\\n    end\\n\\n    User --> Entra\\n    Entra --> App\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## \ud83c\udfdb\ufe0f WAF Pillar Assessment\\n\\n### Overall Scores\\n\\n| Pillar                    | Score | Confidence | Summary                                                                                            |\\n| ------------------------- | ----- | ---------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\\n| \ud83d\udd12 Security               | 8/10  | High       | Entra SSO, Managed Identity, TLS 1.2, Private Endpoints, Key Vault. Gate-acceptance procedure documents the verbatim resume\\nline. Phase 2c extends the contract to every user-facing step agent:\\neach must end its completion path with the same verbatim line so that\\ndirect invocation (`@03-Architect \u2026`) still hands off cleanly.\\n\\nThe verbatim contract is the only token-reduction primitive that\\nactually drops main-agent input tokens, so the lint is hard-fail.\\n\\nWired into:\\n\\n    npm run validate:agents       (via validate-agents.mjs)\\n    npm run validate:orchestrator-handoff   (standalone)\\n    lefthook pre-commit          (via artifact-validation hook)\\n\\nExit codes: 0 on pass, 1 on a missing-or-paraphrased contract,\\n2 on argparse / IO errors.\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\nfrom __future__ import annotations\\n\\nimport argparse\\nimport sys\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\n\\nREPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[2]\\nAGENTS_DIR = REPO_ROOT / \\\".github\\\" / \\\"agents\\\"\\nDEFAULT_AGENT = AGENTS_DIR / \\\"01-orchestrator.agent.md\\\"\\n\\n# Step agents whose completion path must end with the verbatim resume\\n# line (covers direct-invocation flow). Walk cost ~50–100ms per validator × ≥10 redundant walks.\\n   - Design: pre-load step writes `tools/scripts/_cache/workspace-index.json` (agents + skills + instructions + parsed frontmatter). `workspace-index.mjs` gains a `--use-cache` mode that loads from JSON if mtime ≥ scanned files. Higher refactor cost, but eliminates parallel-walk waste entirely.\\n   - Recommend: shared cache (lower risk, smaller refactor). If any of these are unresolvable today, **label §4d as \\\"design proposal — implementation deferred.\\\"**\\n   - **§4e Savings split — CI vs local** (S5): on-disk cache helps local repeat invocations during dev (~100–500 ms per warm run). One-line rationale per item.\\n   - **§3a Skill validators** 2→1 (merge [validate-skill-checks.mjs](tools/scripts/validate-skill-checks.mjs) into [validate-skills.mjs](tools/scripts/validate-skills.mjs)).\\n   - **§3b Model validators** 3→1 (`validate-model.mjs --only=`).\\n   - **§3c Considered but rejected**: workflow-graph (separate gates), IaC contract (schema vs prose), H2/heading cluster (refactor risk > benefit), orphaned-content vs glob-audit (different walk semantics).\\n6. **§4 Performance (secondary — possible bonus from §3 work, not the case for §3)**:\\n   - **§4a Root cause** — `validate:_node` runs N validators via `run-p`. Each is a separate Node.js process. The module-level cache in [workspace-index.mjs](tools/scripts/_lib/workspace-index.mjs) is per-process. At least 9 validators walk the agents tree independently (cite count from Phase 1.3). Stated as a code-shape observation, not a perf complaint.\\n   - **§4b Approach A — single-process mega-validator** (S4 promoted to equal weight): one Node entry-point that requires all validators as modules, shares one in-memory `workspace-index`. Trade-off: validators lose independent runnability. Refactor cost: medium.\\n   - **§4c Approach B — shared on-disk cache** at `tools/scripts/_cache/workspace-index.json`, warmed by a pre-step. Trade-off: invalidation complexity (stale cache, atomic writes). Refactor cost: small per-validator change, larger pre-step.\\n   - **§4d Cache invalidation algorithm — design proposal only.** Mtime check against the newest file across all scanned dirs; atomic write via `fs.writeFileSync(tmp); fs.renameSync(tmp, final)`; CI cache invalidated per workflow run unless `actions/cache` is wired to a content-addressed key (`hashFiles('.github/agents/**', '.github/skills/**', '.github/instructions/**')`). **Implementation deferred and explicitly optional** — the §3 consolidations are justified without it.\\n   - **§4e Reframe** — the section's load-bearing claim is *\\\"after §3 lands, this perf work becomes substantially cheaper to do later.\\\"* Not *\\\"do §3 because perf.\\\"* Phase 1.1 baseline timing is published in the doc as a snapshot; any post-cleanup re-measurement is left to the follow-up PR.\\n7. **§5 Lint configs** — downgraded to \\\"inventory + observations\\\" (review finding S2). Claim non-contradiction **only** for configs read end-to-end in Phase 1.7; otherwise mark \\\"not checked\\\" explicitly. No assertion without evidence.\\n8. **§6 CI vs lefthook duplication map** — confirm two-pass model is intentional. No action.\\n9. **§7 Priority table** — ranked P1/P2/P3 actions with effort/savings/risk grades. Perf items (§4) are P3 at most; consolidation items (§3) are P1/P2.\\n10. **§8 Execution trigger** — **hard 7-day gate**. User opens `chore/retire-orphan-scripts` PR within 7 days of doc landing on `main`, or the doc author re-opens this plan to renegotiate scope. **No \\\"exploratory only\\\" escape hatch** — the sibling [docs/skills-assessment-2026-05-17.md](docs/skills-assessment-2026-05-17.md) used that escape hatch and is now process theater. This doc does not.\\n\\n## Phase 3 — Lint + handoff\\n\\n1. `npm run lint:md` on the new doc.\\n2. Walk cost ~50–100ms per validator × ≥10 redundant walks.\\n   - Design: pre-load step writes `tools/scripts/_cache/workspace-index.json` (agents + skills + instructions + parsed frontmatter). `workspace-index.mjs` gains a `--use-cache` mode that loads from JSON if mtime ≥ scanned files. CI `validate:_node` runs a `prevalidate:cache-warm` step once before `run-p`, then deletes cache after.\\n   - Alternative: mega-validator approach — combine the ~10 agent-reading validators into a single multi-pass process. Higher refactor cost, but eliminates parallel-walk waste entirely.\\n   - Recommend: shared cache (lower risk, smaller refactor). Document mega-validator as alternative.\\n   - **Savings estimate**: 500ms–2s wall-clock per `validate:_node` run. The \\\"~40\\\" approximation is replaced by a real count + the `npm-run-all2` max-parallel default (verify in the installed copy under `node_modules/npm-run-all2/`).\\n4. **Independently verify alias-cluster claims** (review finding M3) — open each canonical script, find the `--only=` parser, list every switch arm:\\n   - [validate-artifacts.mjs](tools/scripts/validate-artifacts.mjs)\\n   - [validate-agents.mjs](tools/scripts/validate-agents.mjs)\\n   - [validate-skills.mjs](tools/scripts/validate-skills.mjs)\\n5. **Confirm orphan candidates** — final grep across `.github/`, `tools/`, `package.json`, `lefthook.yml`, `*.md`, `*.yml`, including these previously-unchecked locations:\\n   - `tools/apex-prompts/`\\n   - `tools/tests/`\\n   - `site/src/content/docs/`\\n   - `agent-output/`\\n   - `.archive/` (if present)\\n   Targets: `markdown-prettifiers/`, `validate-context-budget.mjs`, `strip-handoff-kind.py`, `migrate-legacy-findings.mjs`.\\n6. **Cross-check shared-walk hypothesis** — confirm [validate-deprecated-models.mjs](tools/scripts/validate-deprecated-models.mjs) walks its own tree (does not use [_lib/workspace-index.mjs](tools/scripts/_lib/workspace-index.mjs)). *Already confirmed during exploration; re-cite line number in the doc.*\\n7. **Read every lint config end-to-end** (review finding S2) — `.markdownlint-cli2.jsonc`, `eslint.config.mjs`, `.prettierrc.json`, `.prettierignore`, `.yamllint.yml`, `commitlint.config.js`. The earlier \\\"no contradictions\\\" assertion was inherited from a subagent that admitted only partial reading; the doc must either back the claim with file-line citations or downgrade §5 to \\\"inventory only, contradiction-check deferred.\\\"\\n8. **Decide Python-validator scope** (review finding S1) — explicitly mark in/out of scope:\\n   - `validate_orchestrator_handoff.py`\\n   - `validate_question_batching.py`\\n   - `validate_review_ceiling.py`\\n   - `smoke_verify.py`\\n   - `profile_debug_log.py`\\n   Recommendation: **inventory only** in §3, full retire/consolidate analysis deferred to a sibling plan. State this in the doc's TL;DR.\\n\\n## Phase 2 — Author doc\\n\\nSections mirror the skills-assessment template, with the corrections below.\\n\\n1. **TL;DR** — headline counts (from Phase 1 measurements, not approximations) + the §8 execution trigger up front. Cross-link to [docs/skills-assessment-2026-05-17.md](docs/skills-assessment-2026-05-17.md) explicitly (review finding G2).\\n2. **Inventory** — every `tools/scripts/*` file, every npm script grouped by canonical script invoked, every lint config, every CI workflow + lefthook hook caller, Python validators called out explicitly per Phase 1.8.\\n3. **§1 Retire** — table for the 4 orphan targets, action = `git rm`. Note paired deletion of [.markdownlint-cli2.jsonc](.markdownlint-cli2.jsonc#L174) ignore entry.\\n4. **§2 Dedupe aliases** — artifacts 5→2 (`validate:artifacts` + `fix:artifacts`); agents retire `lint:agent-checks` and `lint:model-alignment`. P3 entry: retire public `check:h2-order` npm script.\\n5. **§3 Consolidate** — selected items + a **\\\"Considered but rejected\\\"** subsection (review finding S7) recording why workflow-graph, IaC contract validators, heading/H2 cluster, and orphaned-content/glob-audit were not merged. One-line rationale per item.\\n   - **§3a Skill validators** 2→1 (merge [validate-skill-checks.mjs](tools/scripts/validate-skill-checks.mjs) into [validate-skills.mjs](tools/scripts/validate-skills.mjs)).\\n   - **§3b Model validators** 3→1 (`validate-model.mjs --only=`).\\n   - **§3c Considered but rejected**: workflow-graph (separate gates), IaC contract (schema vs prose), H2/heading cluster (refactor risk > benefit), orphaned-content vs glob-audit (different walk semantics).\\n6. **§4 Performance** — restructured per review findings M1, S3, S4, S5:\\n   - **§4a Root cause** — `validate:_node` runs N validators via `run-p`. Each is a separate Node.js process. The module-level cache in [workspace-index.mjs](tools/scripts/_lib/workspace-index.mjs) is per-process. At least 9 validators walk the agents tree independently (cite count from Phase 1.3).\\n   - **§4b Approach A — single-process mega-validator** (S4 promoted to equal weight): one Node entry-point that requires all validators as modules, shares one in-memory `workspace-index`. Trade-off: validators lose independent runnability. Refactor cost: medium.\\n   - **§4c Approach B — shared on-disk cache** at `tools/scripts/_cache/workspace-index.json`, warmed by a pre-step. Trade-off: invalidation complexity (stale cache, atomic writes). Refactor cost: small per-validator change, larger pre-step.\\n   - **§4d Cache invalidation algorithm** (review finding S3 — write it concretely or label as design proposal): mtime check against the newest file across all scanned dirs (agents, skills, instructions). Atomic write via `fs.writeFileSync(tmp); fs.renameSync(tmp, final)`. CI cache invalidated per workflow run unless `actions/cache` is wired to a content-addressed key (`hashFiles('.github/agents/**', '.github/skills/**', '.github/instructions/**')`). If any of these are unresolvable today, **label §4d as \\\"design proposal — implementation deferred.\\\"**\\n   - **§4e Savings split — CI vs local** (S5): on-disk cache helps local repeat invocations during dev (~100–500 ms per warm run). On CI, the savings depend entirely on whether `actions/cache` is wired in. Mega-validator approach saves both equally.\\n   - **§4f Honest range** (M1): all savings quoted as a range bracketed by the Phase 1.2 cold-vs-warm microbenchmark. If OS page cache already absorbs the redundant walks, the doc must say so.\\n7. **§5 Lint configs** — downgraded to \\\"inventory + observations\\\" (review finding S2). Claim non-contradiction **only** for configs read end-to-end in Phase 1.7; otherwise mark \\\"not checked\\\" explicitly. No assertion without evidence.\\n8. **§6 CI vs lefthook duplication map** — confirm two-pass model is intentional. No action.\\n9. **§7 Priority table** — ranked P1/P2/P3 actions with effort/savings/risk grades.\\n10. **§8 Execution trigger** (review finding S6 — new section): the doc is **input to a follow-up PR**, not the end state. Concrete gate:\\n    - User opens `chore/retire-orphan-scripts` PR within 7 days, **or**\\n    - Doc is reclassified as \\\"exploratory / not actioned\\\" in its revision footer.\\n    Without §8, the doc joins `skills-assessment-2026-05-17.md` on the unactioned shelf.\\n\\n## Phase 3 — Lint + handoff\\n\\n1. `npm run lint:md` on the new doc.\\n2. Walk cost ~50–100ms per validator × ≥10 redundant walks.\\n   - Design: pre-load step writes `tools/scripts/_cache/workspace-index.json` (agents + skills + instructions + parsed frontmatter). `workspace-index.mjs` gains a `--use-cache` mode that loads from JSON if mtime ≥ scanned files. Higher refactor cost, but eliminates parallel-walk waste entirely.\\n   - Recommend: shared cache (lower risk, smaller refactor). Each is a separate Node.js process. The module-level cache in [workspace-index.mjs](tools/scripts/_lib/workspace-index.mjs) is per-process. At least 9 validators walk the agents tree independently (cite count from Phase 1.3).\\n   - **§4b Approach A — single-process mega-validator** (S4 promoted to equal weight): one Node entry-point that requires all validators as modules, shares one in-memory `workspace-index`. Trade-off: validators lose independent runnability. Refactor cost: medium.\\n   - **§4c Approach B — shared on-disk cache** at `tools/scripts/_cache/workspace-index.json`, warmed by a pre-step. Trade-off: invalidation complexity (stale cache, atomic writes). Refactor cost: small per-validator change, larger pre-step.\\n   - **§4d Cache invalidation algorithm** (review finding S3 — write it concretely or label as design proposal): mtime check against the newest file across all scanned dirs (agents, skills, instructions). Atomic write via `fs.writeFileSync(tmp); fs.renameSync(tmp, final)`. CI cache invalidated per workflow run unless `actions/cache` is wired to a content-addressed key (`hashFiles('.github/agents/**', '.github/skills/**', '.github/instructions/**')`). If any of these are unresolvable today, **label §4d as \\\"design proposal — implementation deferred.\\\"**\\n   - **§4e Savings split — CI vs local** (S5): on-disk cache helps local repeat invocations during dev (~100–500 ms per warm run). On CI, the savings depend entirely on whether `actions/cache` is wired in. Mega-validator approach saves both equally.\\n   - **§4f Honest range** (M1): all savings quoted as a range bracketed by the Phase 1.2 cold-vs-warm microbenchmark. If OS page cache already absorbs the redundant walks, the doc must say so.\\n7. **§5 Lint configs** — downgraded to \\\"inventory + observations\\\" (review finding S2). No GitHub API calls have been invoked.\\n\\n**Abort conditions** (any → halt, report state, exit non-zero):\\n\\n- A Pre-condition fails. Report which one.\\n- Phase 1.1 baseline `time` command fails or yields no wall-clock output.\\n- Phase 1.3 validator count returns 0 (parser failure).\\n- Phase 1.5 final-orphan grep finds a real reference to any candidate (then update plan to remove that candidate from §1; do not abort the doc — only abort if all 4 candidates are disqualified, in which case §1 becomes empty and the doc is no longer worth shipping).\\n- Phase 2 file write fails or produces 0-byte output.\\n- Phase 3 `lint:md` fails after 1 auto-fix retry (`npm run lint:md -- --fix`).\\n- The agent reaches step N+1 without the evidence artifact from step N. Halt before authoring with stale or missing inputs.\\n\\n## Phase 1 — Evidence capture (fully scripted)\\n\\nRun every step from `/workspaces/azure-agentic-infraops`.
